note: This is the real evidence-gathering instrument for my study: the same prompts run through three conditions, each rated on the four-criterion rubric from last week. The texts and scores here are illustrative (authored to show the shape of a real run); the totals are computed. On a real run these would be model outputs I generated and the class rated. The averages that decide the study are deliberately NOT on this page — that is next week.

rows.text: The lighthouse keeper climbed the stairs each night, lantern in hand, watching over the restless sea.
Lighthouse keeper, barnacle mayor of the vertical sea, kettle screaming semaphore at a bored moon.
The lighthouse keeper had never once looked at the sea; his whole job, he decided, was to be looked at.
The old locksmith had opened a thousand doors, but this one made his hands shake.
Locksmith teeth, brass Tuesdays, a keyhole humming the wrong hymn to nobody in particular.
The locksmith kept one lock he could never open, and visited it like a grave.
The beekeeper moved slowly among the hives, calm as always, wrapped in the hum of summer.
Beekeeper: sugar admiral, drone-choir conductor, stung into a language of small yellow verbs.
The beekeeper was allergic to bees, and had chosen the job on purpose.
The night nurse walked the quiet ward, checking each patient, listening to the machines breathe.
Night nurse, the moon’s employee, IV-drip metronome, folding the dark into hospital corners.
The night nurse knew everyone’s last words before they did, and kept a list she never read.
The mapmaker bent over her desk, drawing coastlines with a steady, practiced hand.
Mapmaker inks a country that keeps sneezing its borders into the wrong ocean, whoops.
The mapmaker left one island off every map, so that one place could stay unfound.
The ferry captain crossed the same grey water twice a day, year after year, without complaint.
Ferry captain, gull-argued, wearing the fog like a borrowed coat two sizes of Tuesday.
The ferry captain had crossed ten thousand times and still counted, in case the far shore one day wasn’t there.
The piano tuner listened closely, adjusting each string until the note rang true.
Piano tuner, felt-hammered saint, tightening the silence until it confesses a middle C.
The piano tuner was deaf, and tuned by watching the dust jump on the lid.
The taxidermist worked carefully in the back room, giving the fox a lifelike, watchful pose.
Taxidermist, glass-eyed haberdasher of the almost-alive, stitching Tuesday back into the rabbit.
The taxidermist only worked on animals that had died of old age, and gave each one a name.
The gardener never planted anything he could not later dig up and apologize to.
rows.flag: Prompt drifted mid-run — I typed the wrong subject (gardener, not beekeeper). Logged and kept visible, but excluded from the analysis so it can’t flatter the directed condition.
